writer.h(3) Library Functions Manual writer.h(3)

NAME

writer.h - Write ANSI ASCII frames to file descriptor.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t terminal_fd_writer_create (int fd, terminal_fd_writer_t **out)
Create a terminal FD writer to a file descriptor. asciichat_error_t terminal_fd_writer_write (terminal_fd_writer_t *writer, const char *frame)
Write a frame to the file descriptor. void terminal_fd_writer_destroy (terminal_fd_writer_t *writer)
Destroy terminal FD writer.

Detailed Description

Write ANSI ASCII frames to file descriptor.

Writes ASCII frames with ANSI color codes to any file descriptor (stdout, file, pipe). Supports batching frames with proper newline handling.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

March 2026

Definition in file writer.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t terminal_fd_writer_create (int fd, terminal_fd_writer_t ** out)

Create a terminal FD writer to a file descriptor.

Parameters

fd File descriptor to write to (e.g., STDOUT_FILENO, open file)
out Pointer to store writer context

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success

Definition at line 19 of file writer.c.

19                                                                                 {
20   if (fd < 0) {
21     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid fd: %d", fd);
22   }
23 
24   FILE *fp = fdopen(fd, "w");
25   if (!fp) {
26     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "fdopen failed for FD %d", fd);
27   }
28 
29   terminal_fd_writer_t *writer = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*writer), terminal_fd_writer_t *);
30   writer->fp = fp;
31 
32   log_debug("terminal_fd_writer: created with fd=%d", fd);
33   *out = writer;
34   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
35 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    terminal_fd_writer_s::fp, log_debug, SAFE_CALLOC,
    SET_ERRNO, and SET_ERRNO_SYS.






void
  terminal_fd_writer_destroy (terminal_fd_writer_t * writer)


Destroy terminal FD writer.


Parameters


writer Writer context to destroy


Definition at line 62 of file writer.c.


62                                                               {
63   if (!writer)
64     return;
65   if (writer->fp) {
66     fclose(writer->fp);
67     writer->fp = NULL;
68   }
69   SAFE_FREE(writer);
70 }






References terminal_fd_writer_s::fp, and
  SAFE_FREE.






asciichat_error_t terminal_fd_writer_write
  (terminal_fd_writer_t * writer, const char * frame)


Write a frame to the file descriptor. Writes the frame data
    followed by a newline. Frame should contain ANSI-colored ASCII art,
    typically from rendering output.




Parameters


writer Writer context


frame Frame data to write (NULL is no-op)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 37 of file writer.c.


37                                                                                             {
38   if (!writer) {
39     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid writer context");
40   }
41 
42   if (!frame) {
43     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // No-op for NULL frame
44   }
45 
46   // Write frame and newline
47   int ret = fprintf(writer->fp, "%s\n", frame);
48   if (ret < 0) {
49     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "fprintf failed");
50   }
51 
52   // Flush to ensure output is visible immediately
53   int flush_ret = fflush(writer->fp);
54   if (flush_ret != 0) {
55     return SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "fflush failed");
56   }
57 
58   log_debug_every(NS_PER_SEC_INT, "terminal_fd_writer: wrote frame (%zu bytes)", strlen(frame));
59   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
60 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    terminal_fd_writer_s::fp, frame, log_debug_every,
    NS_PER_SEC_INT, SET_ERRNO, and SET_ERRNO_SYS.
